task.title: Integrating Db2 support with remote COBOL and PL/I programs
task.shortdesc: You can integrate Db2 support into your build procedures for remote COBOL and PL/I programs.

steps.stepsection: To use Db2 precompilation support with remote COBOL and PL/I programs:

step.cmd: Edit the property group that is associated with the remote COBOL or PL/I program.
Click the COBOL or PL/I tab to open the COBOL Settings or PL/I Settings page.
On the Runtime Environments page, select the Db2 checkbox.
step.stepresult: This action enables use of the integrated Db2 coprocessor.

step.cmd: If you want to use a separate Db2 precompiler, do these steps:

substep.cmd: Open the Procedures and Steps page of the COBOL Settings or PL/I Settings.
Select either ELAXFCOP (for COBOL) or ELAXFPLP for PL/I and then click Enable Procedure.

taskbody.postreq: If you are using a separate Db2 precompiler, you can specify the properties for the precompiler by selecting a step under the ELAXFCOP or ELAXFPLP procedure. The Step Options area opens so that you can specify the properties and options to be passed to the Db2 precompiler.
